summary: Branch-based workspace feature testing. Comprehensive backend (25 tests) and frontend (Playwright) testing completed. All branch-scoped features verified working end-to-end. One backend minor gap found: login endpoint missing branch_id in response (but /auth/me provides it, so app works correctly). One backend validation gap: BranchCreate.name has no min_length validator allowing empty-named branches.

minor.issue: Login response user object missing 'branch_id' for department users. BranchContext uses user.branch_id to lock dept users to their branch. Works in practice because AuthContext calls /auth/me immediately after login which does include branch_id.
minor.fix: Add branch_id to the login response user object (same as /auth/me does)

minor.issue: BranchCreate model has no min_length validator for 'name' field. Empty string '' creates a nameless branch. Frontend does validate (toast.error for empty name), but backend doesn't.
minor.fix: Add min_length=1 or @validator to BranchCreate.name in models.py

design_issues.screen: All pages with select dropdowns (Reviews, Settings)
issues: React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be child of <option>/<select>'. Pre-existing issue from Emergent visual editor debug spans injected into select options. NOT a code bug - no user impact. Same as reported in iteration_2.

feature_verification.branch_selector_ba_header: PASS - Shows 'Main Branch' with dropdown chevron (data-testid='branch-selector')
feature_verification.branch_switcher_dropdown_2_branches: PASS - Shows 'Main Branch (Active)' and 'Downtown Branch' with 'Manage Branches...' option
feature_verification.branches_page_at_admin_branches: PASS - Shows both branches with review_count (8/0), post_count (3/0), dept_count (3/0)
feature_verification.create_branch_add_btn: PASS - Add Branch modal opens, creates new branch successfully with success toast
feature_verification.downtown_reviews_0: PASS - After switching to Downtown Branch, Reviews page shows '0 total reviews' and 'No reviews found'
feature_verification.main_branch_reviews_8: PASS - After switching back to Main Branch, Reviews page shows '8 total reviews' with all 8 reviews listed
feature_verification.get_branches_api: PASS - Returns review_count, post_count, dept_count fields
feature_verification.reports_summary_main_branch: PASS - Returns total=8, correct avg_rating and response_rate
feature_verification.reports_summary_downtown_branch: PASS - Returns total=0 for downtown branch
feature_verification.dept_user_branch_badge: PASS - frontdesk@grandhotel.com sees branch-badge (not branch-selector) showing 'Main Branch'
feature_verification.dept_user_cannot_switch: PASS - Branch selector dropdown NOT visible for dept user
feature_verification.dept_user_no_branches_nav: PASS - Branches sidebar nav item hidden for dept user
feature_verification.settings_branch_configuration_subtitle: PASS - Shows 'Main Branch · Branch Configuration' as page subtitle
feature_verification.ba_branch_selector_visible: PASS - branch-selector visible in header for BA with 2+ branches

action_items: LOW PRIORITY: Add branch_id to POST /api/auth/login response user object (auth_routes.py line 38-48) - matches what /auth/me already returns
LOW PRIORITY: Add min_length=1 or a validator to BranchCreate.name field in models.py to prevent empty-named branches via direct API calls
critical_code_review_comments: Login endpoint (auth_routes.py) constructs user response manually and omits branch_id. /auth/me correctly returns full DB user. Minor inconsistency but works because AuthContext calls /auth/me on startup.
BranchCreate model in models.py has name: str with no min_length constraint. Frontend validates but backend doesn't reject empty names.
BranchContext correctly handles dept user branch locking using user.branch_id from /auth/me response.
canSwitchBranch logic (Layout.jsx line 77) correctly checks user.role === 'business_admin' && branches.length > 1 - verified working.
Branch migration in server.py startup correctly assigns location_id to departments and branch_id to dept users.

success_rate.backend: 24/25 (96%) - 1 minor failure: login endpoint missing branch_id in response

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted 2 test branches during testing: 'TEST_Playwright Branch' (Playwright test) and empty-named branch (accidental backend test). Both deleted. DB restored to 2 branches: Main Branch (8 reviews) + Downtown Branch (0 reviews).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Branch feature fully implemented and tested. Grand Hotel has Main Branch (UUID: 6edd50b6-b316-4103-b1e2-593e768ae9b3, 8 reviews, 3 posts, 3 depts) and Downtown Branch (UUID: af9b322e-d4d2-4d69-8a5f-cb72487416db, 0 data). Branch selector works for BA, branch badge for dept users. All branch-scoped APIs work. Two minor gaps: (1) login endpoint missing branch_id, (2) BranchCreate missing name validation.

login_missing_branch_id.root_cause: auth_routes.py POST /login manually constructs user response dict (lines 38-48) and only includes id, email, name, role, client_id, department_id, client. branch_id field from DB user is not added.
login_missing_branch_id.impact: On fresh login, user.branch_id is null until /auth/me is called. BranchContext falls back to first branch (data[0]) which coincidentally is Main Branch. Works in practice.
login_missing_branch_id.fix: Add 'branch_id': user.get('branch_id') to login response user dict

empty_branch_name.root_cause: BranchCreate Pydantic model uses 'name: str' without min_length constraint. Pydantic accepts empty strings for str fields.
empty_branch_name.impact: Empty-named branches can be created via API direct calls, showing as nameless cards in UI.
empty_branch_name.fix: Change to 'name: str = Field(min_length=1)' or add @field_validator
